items.title: Police bust crypto scam call centre in Batu Ferringhi, 5 foreigners nabbed
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have busted a cryptocurrency investment scam call centre targeting Chinese nationals in a raid at Batu Ferringhi, arresting five foreigners. State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the operation by the Northeast District Police Commercial Crime Investigation Department yesterday led to the raid on premises in Batu Ferringhi. He said five foreigners – three men and two women aged between 24 and 46 – were detained for allegedly running an online call centre linked to fictitious USDT cryptocurrency investment and exchange scams.

items.title: Hyundai Malaysia launches Ioniq 5 N, Ioniq 6 N high-performance EVs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hyundai Motor Malaysia has launched the high-performance electric vehicles (EVs) Ionic 5 N and Ionic 6 N, with prices starting from RM443,888 and RM449,888 respectively. "The Hyundai Ionic 5 N and Ionic 6 N are testaments that electric performance vehicles can be equally thrilling and more. The model also offers a WLTP driving range of up to 448km and features Hyundai's N-exclusive technologies, including N e-Shift, N active sound+, N drift optimiser, N torque distribution and N launch control.

items.title: Malaysia eyes RM6.1bil oil and gas services opportunities from Middle East rebuild
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is eyeing up to US$1.5 billion (about RM6.1 billion) in potential procurement opportunities for local oil and gas services and equipment (OGSE) companies from the rebuilding of energy infrastructure in the Middle East. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the rebuilding and repair of oil, gas and petrochemical infrastructure in the region was expected to involve high costs, estimated between US$42 billion (RM170 billion) and US$57 billion (RM231 billion). Akmal said this presented realistic opportunities for Malaysian OGSE companies.

items.title: GWM Malaysia unveils new hybrid SUVs at KLIMS 2026
items.summary: GWM Malaysia has previewed two new electrified models - the Ora 5 HEV and Haval H7 Hi4 PHEV - at the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS) 2026, as the automaker expands its hybrid and plug-in hybrid offerings. The models form part of GWM Malaysia's broader strategy to position its Ora and Haval brands with distinct roles, targeting different customer needs ranging from urban mobility to SUV capability. ORA Expands Into Hybrid Segment The Ora 5 HEV is positioned as a compact crossover aimed at urban users, powered by GWM's new Hi2 intelligent hybrid system.

items.title: Activist warns Rohingya issue could affect Malaysia's global image
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya issue can no longer be viewed solely through a humanitarian lens, but must also take into account national interests, social security and the perception of foreign visitors, a community activist said. Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) public relations chief Daniel Khoo said a clearer and more comprehensive government plan is needed to manage issues involving the Rohingya community in Malaysia, in order to safeguard the country's image as a tourism and investment destination. He said public concern over the issue was growing, particularly due to the presence of Rohingya individuals, including children, seeking donations and begging in tourist hotspots such as the area around the Petronas Twin Towers, Bukit Bintang and other key locations.

items.title: Capital A says no financial hit from Singapore share seizure
items.summary: The seizure involves shares in Teleport Everywhere Pte Ltd and BigPay Pte Ltd held through Move Digital Sdn Bhd. In a Bursa Malaysia reply to query, the group said the notice covers only Move Digital's direct shareholdings, comprising 11.45 per cent in Teleport Everywhere and 99.56 per cent in BigPay. Capital A said it owns 76.86 per cent of Teleport Everywhere through a combination of its direct stake of 65.41 per cent and Move Digital's 11.45 per cent stake, while it holds 99.56 per cent of BigPay through Move Digital.

items.title: Four nabbed in Butterworth call centre scam targeting Singaporeans
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Police have dismantled a call centre-linked investment scam syndicate targeting Singaporeans in a raid here, arresting four men. The operation yesterday was conducted following intelligence from a special operation involving the Bukit Aman Commercial Criminal Investigation Department (CCID) and its Seberang Prai Utara counterpart, at a premises in Butterworth. State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the premises was being used as a call centre to promote non-existent investment schemes targeting victims in Singapore through social media platform "Tantan."

items.title: Rafizi: Na'imah not Bersama's 'moneybags', I am main funder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersama de facto leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that the party received funds linked to the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin's widow. He said Toh Puan Na'imah Abdul Khalid was not the party's financier, adding that he remained Bersama's largest funder at present. During a Facebook Live session today, Rafizi said he personally paid RM30,000 for the party's launch last month and an additional RM50,000 deposit for its headquarters in Puchong.

items.title: Kerjaya Prospek, RAC ink Prai land development pact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd has signed a development agreement through its joint venture Rivanis Ventures Sdn Bhd with Railway Assets Corp (RAC) to buy and develop a 14.1-hectare plot in Prai, Penang. The acquisition and development is estimated at RM156.49 million, comprising a cash payment of RM54.1 million and 338 affordable housing units valued at RM102.39 million. The development land is in Prai, Seberang Perai Tengah, Penang, near key commercial and residential hubs including Butterworth, Bukit Mertajam and Penang Island.

items.title: Two Malaysian peacekeepers wounded in airstrike in Lebanon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysian peacekeepers were lightly wounded in an airstrike in Lebanon today. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the two members of the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 were part of a logistics convoy about 1km west of the United Nations base in Tibnin when an airstrike occurred. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Weststar to supply 300 electric vans to Yinson GreenTech
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Weststar Group, through its automotive arm Weststar Maxus Sdn Bhd, has signed an agreement with Yinson GreenTech to supply 300 electric commercial vans. Weststar group managing director Tan Sri Dr Syed Azman Syed Ibrahim said sustainability has become a business imperative, particularly in the commercial transportation sector. Weststar has already deployed hundreds of Maxus eDeliver 3 and eDeliver 5 electric vans to logistics operators nationwide.

items.title: High Court acquits neurologist of outraging modesty, finds 'serious prejudice' in trial
items.summary: Judicial Commissioner Samry Masri ruled that the amendment of the original charge from Section 377D of the Penal Code for outraging decency to Section 354 which was amended at the end of the prosecution's case had caused serious prejudice to the doctor's defence and rendered the conviction unsafe. However, at the end of the prosecution's case, the trial court found that the original charge could not be established and amended it to a charge under Section 354 before calling for defence and convicted him. Samry held that the amendment fundamentally altered the nature of the charge and caused actual and serious prejudice to the appellant.

items.title: Govt watching 'albino light' trend amid safety concerns - Loke
items.summary: NILAI: Vehicle owners have been reminded that the installation of 'lampu albino', a term commonly used to describe high-intensity white aftermarket LED lighting systems, has not received approval from the Road Transport Department (RTD). Transport Minister Anthony Loke said enforcement action could be taken if the lights are found to endanger other road users. "Currently, no technical approval has been given for such lighting systems.

items.title: Ringgit closes slightly higher against US dollar, major currencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended slightly higher against the greenback and other major currencies on Thursday, shrugging off the impact of stronger-than-expected US inflation data for May. At 6pm, the local note inched up to 4.0650/0690 versus the greenback from Wednesday's close of 4.0670/0715. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit held steady at the RM4.06 level despite the US consumer price index (CPI) hitting a three-year high in May at 4.2 per cent.

items.title: Titijaya, Permodalan Negeri Selangor ink consortium agreement to undertake RM343mil housing project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Titijaya Land Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary, NPO Builders Sdn Bhd has entered into a consortium agreement with PNSB Construction Sdn Bhd (PCSB) to undertake an affordable housing project in Selangor. PCSB is a wholly owned subsidiary of Permodalan Negeri Selangor Bhd (PNSB). "The consortium combines PCSB's strategic capabilities at the state level with NPO Builders' expertise in property development.

items.title: Police cripple drug syndicate in Seberang Prai, seize RM1.2mil MDMA
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police have crippled a drug syndicate following the arrest of three suspects and the seizure of MDMA worth about RM1.2 million in Seberang Prai. State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said two men and a woman, aged between 24 and 33, were arrested in Seberang Prai Tengah. Azizee said authorities also seized assets under the Dangerous Drugs (Forfeiture of Property) Act 1988, including two cars – a Honda City worth RM45,000 and a Kia K2 worth RM35,000.

items.title: Economy minister warns El Nino could cut rainfall by up to 60pc and impact economy
items.summary: Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the phenomenon could bring hotter weather and a sharp reduction in rainfall of between 40 and 60 per cent in several states. He said past El Niño events had shown how severe the consequences could be for both the economy and public services. “Overall, key commodity sectors may experience yield reductions of around 8 to 10 per cent during an El Niño event,” he said.

items.title: Economy minister: RM200 advance paddy incentive reaches nearly 180,000 farmers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government has introduced a one-off advance ploughing incentive of RM200 per hectare for paddy farmers in Peninsular Malaysia to support preparations for the first paddy planting season of 2026. He said the payments, which began in phases on May 19, are aimed at reducing farmers’ initial capital burden and accelerating early planting preparations. “To date, 90 per cent, or nearly 180,000 farmers, have received the payment,” he said during an online global supply crisis briefing today.

items.title: Job losses rise 8.4pc in May amid global supply pressures, says Akmal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Job losses in Malaysia rose by 8.4 per cent in May to 7,766 cases, amid global supply pressures linked to conflict in the Middle East, says Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said data from the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) showed 7,766 workers were recorded as having lost their jobs in May, compared with 7,162 in April. "MyFutureJobs data showed placements rose by 19 per cent, from 12,119 in April to 14,366 in May," he said during a livestreamed briefing on the energy crisis today.

items.title: Remains of 27 Rohingya boat tragedy victims buried in Kedah
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: The remains of 27 Rohingya victims who died in a boat capsize off Langkawi in November last year were laid to rest at the Old Islamic Cemetery in Tualang, Pokok Sena, today. The victims, comprising 13 men and 14 women, were buried at 7.30am in accordance with Islamic rites under the supervision of the Kedah Islamic Religious Council (MAIK). Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the burial was carried out after the management of the remains, which began at 10pm yesterday, was completed at 7am today.

items.title: Malaysia strengthens digital education with first AI powered classroom
items.summary: KANGAR: Malaysia has launched its first AI-powered classroom at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Kuala Perlis, marking a new step in the country's digital education push under the Pre-Google Reference Institution (PGRI) programme. The initiative, part of the Pre-Google Reference Institution (PGRI) programme, is being implemented to strengthen the country's digital education transformation through an AI-powered classroom model. "Perlis is the first state in Malaysia to implement the initiative involving SMK Kuala Perlis, thus paving the way for the expansion of the AI-based learning model to other schools nationwide," he said after the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail graced and officiated the launch of the AI Powered Classroom initiative at SMK Kuala Perlis.

items.title: Hadi: Bersatu tried to weaken, undermine Pas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has labelled his party's partnership with Bersatu as the "loosest" alliance it has ever experienced, accusing the fellow Perikatan Nasional founding member of prioritising power over Muslim unity. He said Bersatu attempted to control the Muslim majority alongside non-Muslim parties while completely disregarding the status of Islam and the Malay-Bumiputera community. "Bersatu tried to weaken Pas' role in pioneering political change and make a fool out of Pas by blocking the entry of more parties into the coalition," he said.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 11, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended slightly higher today, with the FBM KLCI staying in positive territory in the afternoon despite mixed regional markets. The market stayed resilient as investors continued to favour defensive, domestically focused stocks amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and a cautious external outlook. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Home Ministry rolls out refugee registration, status determination process
items.summary: In a statement today, the ministry said the move was crucial to safeguard internal security and improve management of the country's migration affairs. It said that while Malaysia had seen a growing presence of refugees and United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders for decades, comprehensive data on identities and backgrounds remained incomplete. The ministry said the process would help assess security, health and economic implications using verifiable data, while also strengthening migration policies and enforcement related to refugees and UNHCR cardholders.

items.title: Malaysia’s medicine and medical device supply stays stable amid global pressures, says economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the supply of medicines and medical devices in Malaysia remains stable, with most items classified as low risk. He said the government will continue strengthening buffer stocks and expanding supply sources when necessary. “The supply of medicines and medical devices remains generally stable.

items.title: State govts urged to build innovation ecosystems to boost start-ups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Science, Technology and Innovation Ministry (Mosti) has proposed that all state governments establish their own innovation ecosystems to accelerate the development of start-ups nationwide. Its minister Chang Lih Kang said federal agencies were ready to extend cooperation and support should state governments pursue the initiative. He said the collaboration between the Selangor Information Technology and Digital Economy Corporation (Sidec) and the Malaysian Research Accelerator for Technology and Innovation (MRANTI) under the Selangor Triple Accelerator 2026 programme was a strong example of linking state initiatives with national platforms.

items.title: KPDN seizes 6,000 litres of diesel in Melaka raid under Op Tiris 4.0 over suspected subsidy diversion
items.summary: MELAKA, June 11 — The Melaka Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 6,000 litres of diesel believed to have been diverted in an Integrated Op Tiris 4.0 raid on a premises in Taman IKS Paya Rumput here last night. Its director Mohd Hazimin Jamaludin said also seized in the raid at 9 pm last night were a lorry, an additional tank with a capacity of 3,000 litres, two skid tanks, two fuel cards, several fuel pumps and a flow meter estimated to be worth a total of RM106,345. He said the case was being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961 for violating Subregulation 21(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974, namely carrying out wholesale, retail, or manufacturing transactions of any scheduled goods (diesel) without a valid license from the director of Supply Control.

items.title: DBKL, agencies join forces against unlicensed foreign traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: City Hall (DBKL) will establish a multi-agency task force to address the growing presence of foreign traders in the city. Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said a decision to step up enforcement against foreign traders and hawkers in the city was made at today's Special Task Force on Agency Reform (Star) meeting. Speaking after the announcement of Backlane Upgrading Works at Desa Sri Hartamas today, Fadlun said DBKL had conducted more than 4,000 enforcement operations targeting business activities at markets across the city.

items.title: Govt braces for El Niño as energy crisis risks deepen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is preparing for the possible impact of El Niño, which could intensify pressure on food, water and energy supplies amid the ongoing global energy crisis triggered by tensions in the Middle East. He said El Niño could bring hotter and drier conditions, with rainfall in several states potentially declining by between 40 and 60 per cent. "This phenomenon could also add pressure to the ongoing energy crisis, and the matter has been brought to the attention of NEAC," he said during a live-streamed briefing on the energy crisis today.

items.title: Remains of 27 Rohingya migrants who died in boat capsize laid to rest in Kedah
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 11 — The remains of 27 Rohingya victims from Myanmar who died in a boat capsize incident last year were laid to rest at the Kampung Tualang Islamic Cemetery near Pokok Sena today. Kedah Police Chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the remains of 13 men and 14 women who died in the tragic incident was recovered in Langkawi waters last year. “The unfortunate incident involved a group of migrants attempting to enter the country’s waters illegally.

items.title: Disciplinary hair check led to confrontation at school – Perak Education Dept
items.summary: IPOH: The state Education Department has clarified that an incident involving a male discipline teacher and several students at a secondary school in Kuala Kangsar was triggered by provocation during a hair inspection exercise. Its director Zulkafli Mohamed Mokhtar said the incident at 7.50am yesterday occurred during a disciplinary check on students' appearance at the school's multipurpose hall. "However, three students refused to comply and responded in a rude tone," he said when contacted today.

items.title: Malaysia, Turkmenistan to seal hydrocarbons partnership on June 19 as energy security push intensifies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Malaysia and Turkmenistan are expected to sign a long-term hydrocarbons cooperation agreement on June 19 as part of efforts to strengthen the country’s energy supply chain, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. “This also includes cooperation through the Asia Zero Emission Community (AZEC), Partnership on Wide Energy and Resources Resilience Asia (POWERR Asia), energy cooperation with Australia, as well as a long-term hydrocarbons partnership with Turkmenistan, which is expected to be signed on June 19, 2026,” said Akmal Nasrullah during a briefing on the global supply crisis streamed on the ministry’s Facebook page today. “With a strong economic foundation, a strategic energy position and data-driven economic management, Malaysia is not only capable of managing global uncertainties but is also well-positioned to move forward with greater confidence,” said Akmal Nasrullah.

items.title: Pro-Net unveils upgraded e.Mas 7 Premium Plus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PROTON Holdings Bhd's electric vehicle unit Pro-Net today unveiled the Proton e.Mas 7 Premium Plus, an upgraded variant of Malaysia's first national EV priced from RM125,800. The variant offers a larger battery, extended driving range and enhanced comfort features. "By combining greater driving range with enhanced comfort, the Premium Plus makes EV ownership even more practical and enjoyable.

items.title: In ageing South Korea, AI dolls care for the elderly
items.summary: IN her tiny apartment in South Korea, where she lives alone, 78-year-old Bang Chun-ja spends her days with a childlike AI-powered doll she says she prefers to people. The doll "only makes me laugh", she added. [[nid:1460813]] Developing the doll involved years of field research, said company head Kim Ji-hee.

items.title: Perak plans RM38.7mil observatory to boost astronomy, moon-sighting research
items.summary: IPOH: The Perak government is planning to develop a RM38.65 million state observatory in Manjung to strengthen falak (Islamic astronomy) activities and expand astronomy education and research. "To strengthen existing facilities and expand falak and astronomy activities, the state government is planning the development of the Perak State Observatory," he said after launching Perak Astronomy Month at a hotel here today. Saarani said the proposed observatory would occupy a 3.8-hectare site at Pantai Pasir Panjang and serve as the state's official falak observation centre.

items.title: FMM calls for timely intervention to avoid long-term manufacturing risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Manufacturers require timely and targeted intervention to prevent temporary disruptions from becoming longer-term structural disadvantages for Malaysian manufacturers, according to the Federation of Malaysian Manufacturers (FMM). "Manufacturing remains a key pillar of the Malaysian economy, contributing significantly to exports, employment, investment and industrial development. "Measures that help manufacturers manage rising costs, secure critical inputs, maintain liquidity and preserve market competitiveness will be essential to protecting industrial capacity and investor confidence," he said in a statement today.

items.title: MCW seeks transparent probe into alleged discrepancies in petroleum cargo movement at Tanjung Langsat Port
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, Nov 11 — Malaysia Corruption Watch (MCW) has urged the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to investigate allegations of discrepancies between bills of lading and the physical movement of petroleum cargo at the Tanjung Langsat Port in Johor, involving transactions said to be worth millions of ringgit. MCW president Jais Abdul Karim said if the claims of inconsistencies between shipping documentation and actual cargo movement are substantiated, they could open the door to various forms of misconduct. In this regard, MCW called on authorities to strengthen cross-check mechanisms between port data, Customs records, vessel monitoring systems, trade documents and terminal storage records.

items.title: Perak govt to repair village bridge where missing hiker Jaslinda was found
items.summary: IPOH, June 11 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the state government will repair the dilapidated wooden bridge in Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, where missing female hiker Jaslinda Saludin was found. Saarani said the state government had received applications from the relevant parties, including the Orang Asli community, to repair the bridge. Saarani said the Public Works Department (PWD) and the State Economic Planning Unit (UPEN) would first assess the justification for repairing the bridge.

items.title: Back-to-back Sabah raids uncover ‘mini zoo’ and suspected wildlife meat trade
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 11 — Sabah wildlife authorities have seized a range of protected animals and wildlife products in two separate enforcement operations in Penampang and Tawau, detaining two local men suspected of possessing and trading wildlife without valid permits. — Picture courtesy of Sabah Wildlife Department The operation, conducted by the Wildlife Crime Bureau (WCB) Special Investigation Intelligence Unit together with the Sabah Wildlife Department, found a refrigerator believed to contain parts of protected wildlife, including venison, barking deer meat and porcupine meat. The suspect was arrested to assist investigations under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for allegedly possessing protected wildlife and wildlife products without valid permits or documentation.

items.title: PAC to summon Defence Ministry for clarification on Norway’s revocation of Naval Strike Missile licence
items.summary: SEPANG, June 11 — The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) will summon the Ministry of Defence (Mindef) to provide clarification and an update on Norway’s decision to revoke an export licence for Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) intended for Malaysia. PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the matter would be discussed during a briefing on June 23, which will also involve the Parliamentary Special Select Committee on Security. Therefore, we will call Mindef to PAC to provide clarification and a briefing,” she told reporters after a PAC working visit to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today.

items.title: Authorities confirm no injuries after chemical leak at Pasir Gudang factory, monitoring continues
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 11 — A 200-litre industrial tank containing a chemical known as ‘Spent Inorganic Acid’ (SW206) leaked at a factory in Jalan Keluli, Pasir Gudang, this morning. Senior operations commander Deputy Fire Superintendent II Firdaus Ahmad said the department received an emergency call at 11.59 am and a Fire Rescue Tender with six personnel from the Pasir Gudang Fire and Rescue Station was dispatched to the scene. He said the Operations Response Team (PKO) carried out the operation together with the Department of Environment and monitored the situation at the location.

items.title: Bottled water shipment of 6,000 cartons cleared in Miri easing supply disruption from pipeline repairs
items.summary: MIRI, June 11 — A total of 6,000 cartons of bottled water has been brought into Miri to help meet the needs of residents affected by water supply disruptions caused by repair works on a leaking main pipeline at Mile 7, Jalan Miri–Bintulu, said Sarawak Transport Minister Datuk Seri Lee Kim Shin He said the shipment of bottled water has been fully cleared and is currently being distributed to the market to ensure sufficient supply for public use. Lee added that the Miri Port Authority and the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) provided full cooperation by expediting the clearance process for containers carrying the bottled water supplies into Miri. He also advised bottled water retailers not to take advantage of the situation by increasing prices during the period of water supply disruption.

items.title: Non-Malays’ frustration and anger — William Leong Jee Keen
items.summary: JUNE 11 — Non-Malays are frustrated and angry with PMX and PH. The disappointment is not because Anwar Ibrahim and PH won GE15 to form the government. It is because Anwar Ibrahim and PH did not win GE15 but formed a government.

items.title: AirBorneo outlines urgent measures to restore flight operations in Sarawak and Sabah after recent disruptions
items.summary: KUCHING, June 11 — AirBorneo has outlined a series of measures to strengthen fleet reliability and restore normal flight operations following recent disruptions affecting services in Sarawak and Sabah. In a statement, the Sarawak-owned airline said it understood the inconvenience caused to passengers and was working closely with its maintenance partners to expedite repairs and return affected aircraft to service as soon as possible. It said its maintenance teams and partners were intensifying efforts to complete the required work, while ensuring all aircraft meet strict operational and safety standards before returning to service.

items.title: RM9m in alleged false claims: MACC remands 73 agents, company owners in Daya Kerjaya probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 73 individuals, comprising agents and company owners, on suspicion of falsifying Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive claims worth about RM9 million. The source said MACC also recorded 104 statements during the operation, involving 32 persons under investigation and 72 witnesses. “Through this operation, MACC also froze 25 company bank accounts containing about RM390,000,” the source said.

items.title: Penang High Court upholds dog‑beating convictions, reduces fines from RM50,000 to RM30,000 each
items.summary: Justice Choong Yeow Choy dismissed Rosli Mat Zin and Suhaili Haron’s appeal against their conviction under Section 29 (1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, finding that the conviction was grounded on admissible direct evidence and properly admitted video evidence. However, he allowed their appeal against the sentence and varied the RM50,000 fine in default 12 months’ imprisonment imposed by the Sessions Court with a fine of RM30,000 each, in default six months’ imprisonment. He said that no provision in the Animal Welfare Act restricted the police from handling cases arising under the Act.

items.title: Deputy IGP says fentanyl‑based ‘Piu Piu’ leaves users zombie‑like, calls for total vape ban
items.summary: Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the drug, a mixture of fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, could cause severe intoxication effects, leaving users in a zombie-like state. He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed vape usage as having deviated from its original purpose and being widely abused by drug trafficking syndicates as a modus operandi to evade detection by the authorities. Earlier Ayob Khan officiated a hospital visit programme held in conjunction with the 219th Police Day celebration at Hospital Tunku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani, the Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia Specialist Children’s Hospital (HPKK UKM), here.

items.title: Ampang English teacher pleads not guilty to charges of sexually assaulting nine‑year‑old pupil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court to two charges of committing physical sexual assault against a female pupil last month. Mohd Saufi Jama’in, 45, is accused of committing the acts against a nine-year-old girl, with whom the accused had a relationship of trust as her English teacher. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin offered bail of RM20,000 with one surety for both charges, along with additional conditions that the accused must report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from contacting or disturbing the victim and tampering with witnesses.

items.title: Perak police continue probe despite apology over TikTok claims defaming Tok Batin, hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: IPOH, June 11 — Police will continue investigating a man who allegedly posted defamatory content about the Tok Batin of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, linked to the discovery of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, despite his public apology. Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said investigations will be conducted under Section 500 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, following 11 police reports lodged by various parties, including a group of Tok Batin, over the issue. Yesterday, the man issued a public apology via a video recording following controversy over a livestream on his TikTok account, which allegedly defamed Mohd Asmmadi and Jaslinda.

items.title: Rohingya man charged with murder after fellow countryman stabbed to death in Seberang Perai
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, June 11 — A Rohingya man was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his fellow countryman in a residential area last month. Osman Bawdi Alom, 38, nodded to indicate he understood after the charge was read in Malay before Magistrate Nurul Aina Ahmad. He is accused of murdering Abdul Rahman Shah Mia, 31, at Lorong Belibis 14, Taman Belibis, South Seberang Perai, here on May 31.

items.title: Man, 67, remanded in Batu Pahat over alleged rape of 13-year-old granddaughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A 67-year-old man has reportedly been remanded for six days in Batu Pahat on suspicion of raping his 13-year-old granddaughter last year. According to Utusan Malaysia , Batu Pahat district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani confirmed that police had arrested the elderly suspect, who is now under remand. Police reportedly received a report two days ago after the victim told her mother about the alleged abuse.

items.title: Driver found dead after Toyota Innova bursts into flames in early‑morning crash with trailer near Pantai Remis
items.summary: IPOH, June 11 — A driver was found dead after the vehicle he was travelling in caught fire following a crash involving a sport utility vehicle (SUV) and a trailer lorry along Jalan Batu 9, Kampung Semangat, Segari, near Pantai Remis early this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.36am, prompting a team from the Pantai Remis Fire and Rescue Station to be dispatched to the scene. “After the fire was extinguished, rescue personnel discovered a victim trapped inside the vehicle.

items.title: Caught with 490 turtle eggs, man gets 18 months’ jail and RM50,000 fine in Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A foreign man has reportedly been sentenced to 18 months’ jail and fined RM50,000 after pleading guilty to possessing 490 turtle eggs from fully protected species in Sabah. According to Sinar Harian , the accused, Manan Saibung, who had no identification documents, admitted to the charge before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali in Sandakan on Thursday. He was reportedly found with eggs from the green turtle (Chelonia mydas) and hawksbill turtle (Eretmochelys imbricata) species without valid permission.

items.title: Unemployed man charged with murder after acquaintance found dead in rolled‑up mattress in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA, June 11 — An unemployed man was charged at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of an acquaintance at a shophouse in March. Kamil Harris Mohd Abd Nasir, 40, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. He is accused of murdering Mohd Idris Yasmir, 40, at Taman Seri Pandan 1, Jalan Pokok Mangga here between 10pm, March 30 and 3am, March 31.

items.title: Students unhappy over haircut, discipline teacher allegedly ends up punched in Kuala Kangsar
items.summary: The incident, which occurred around 11.30am, is said to have been triggered when the teacher cut the hair of one of the students involved, Sinar Harian (SH) reported. Perak executive councillor for education, higher learning, youth and sports Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said the state government is awaiting an official report from the Perak Education Department (JPN). Khairuddin added that students must respect their teachers even if certain aspects of the learning process may be disagreeable.

items.title: No more cash counters: EPF moves contributions to apps, banks and self‑service terminals from July 1
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 11 — The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) will discontinue remittance counters at all branches nationwide from July 1, 2026, shifting contribution payments fully to digital and alternative channels. The retirement fund said other service counters would continue operating as usual, The Star reported. The EPF stressed that the closure of receipting counters would not affect members’ or employers’ ability to make payments, as multiple alternative channels remain available.

items.title: Knife‑wielding couple nabbed after Grab driver’s throat slashed at Kota Tinggi bus terminal
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 11 — Police arrested a couple after they were believed to be involved in a knife-wielding robbery of a ride-sharing driver at the Tun Sri Lanang bus terminal here yesterday. Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the couple, both aged 20, were nabbed in separate locations by policemen from the Criminal Investigation Division (CID) yesterday. “Police also seized a knife, suspected to be the weapon used in the robbery attempt, from the couple,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Bersatu vows to defend Endau seat as Alwiyah Talib exits, Azmin rallies grassroots support
items.summary: Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali said Endau remains the party’s stronghold and grassroots support is still intact. “Mersing still remains a Bersatu stronghold. “The Mersing parliamentary seat and Endau state seat were won by Bersatu.

items.title: Police report filed after Albert Tei stages four protests at Menara Komunikasi, J‑KOM cites obstruction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 11 — The Community Communications Department (J-KOM) today lodged a police report against activist Albert Tei over allegations that he had disrupted operations at the Ministry of Communications through a series of gatherings held since last month. J-KOM director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali said the report was lodged after the individual allegedly staged several gatherings in front of the ministry building, affecting the smooth conduct of duties by civil servants. He said the report to MCMC would involve Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, which concerns the improper use of network facilities or network services to harass or provoke others.

items.title: Malaysia’s jobless rate edges back to 3.0pc in April, 511,800 unemployed as labour force grows
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of employed persons continued to increase in April 2026, rising by 0.1 per cent to 16.82 million persons against the previous month (March 2026: 16.80 million persons). “The employees’ category constituted 75.0 per cent of total employed persons in April 2026. This category increased marginally by 0.05 per cent to 12.61 million persons (March 2026: 12.60 million persons).
